---
title: "Hackathon Volunteer Guide"
format: 
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    toc: true
---

{{< include "event_details_agenda.qmd" >}}

## **What to Expect During the Hackathon**

Hackathons are dynamic, high-energy events where teams collaborate to solve real-world challenges in a short time. As a volunteer, your support is essential to helping participants stay focused, motivated, and productive. This guide outlines what to expect and how you can contribute, depending on your role.

We asked participants to post their questions in the [Help and support](https://teams.microsoft.com/l/channel/19%3A5a57b51811a74da694ee272057caebac%40thread.tacv2/Help%20and%20support?groupId=620ed7ec-9dc9-4ca1-bc7b-848f6bd80878&tenantId=fad277c9-c60a-4da1-b5f3-b3b8b34a82f9) channel on [Pre-stats awayday hackathon](https://teams.microsoft.com/l/team/19%3AmDwTrFC1t5hhuDsXfajF516hmOWTFGjgIvJcPjdCLNM1%40thread.tacv2/conversations?groupId=620ed7ec-9dc9-4ca1-bc7b-848f6bd80878&tenantId=fad277c9-c60a-4da1-b5f3-b3b8b34a82f9) on Teams. Please monitor the channel and try to answer their questions as soon as possible.

### 🧠 Data Experts

**Your role:** Help teams understand and work effectively with the data provided.

**What to expect:**

-   Teams may ask for help interpreting datasets, understanding data limitations, or identifying relevant variables.

-   You might be asked to validate assumptions or suggest ways to clean or transform data.

-   Be ready to explain domain-specific concepts or provide context that helps teams build meaningful solutions.

-   You may be pulled into conversations about ethics, bias, or data sensitivity (your input here is especially valuable).

**Tips:**

-   Familiarize yourself with the datasets ahead of time.

-   Encourage teams to document their data decisions.

-   Be available to answer questions on teams.

{{< include "data_volunteer_list.qmd" >}}

### 💻 Tech Volunteers

**Your role:** Provide technical support and troubleshooting for teams.

**What to expect:**

-   Teams may need help with coding issues, software setup, version control, or integrating APIs.

-   You might assist with debugging, optimization, or helping teams choose the right tools.

-   Some of you will be running a drop-in session both days of the hackathon. Please see the [Agenda section](#agenda) for more detail.

**Tips:**

-   Have your laptop ready to jump into code.

-   Be patient and supportive as some participants may be new to certain technologies.

-   Share best practices when you see opportunities to improve team workflows.

{{< include "tech_volunteer_list.qmd" >}}

### 🤝 Team Support Volunteers

**Your role:** Check in on teams to ensure they’re doing well and have what they need.

**What to expect:**

-   You’ll check on team progress, morale, and needs. This can be by dropping the team a message or checking in with them in person.

-   You may help teams clarify their goals, manage time, or connect with other volunteers.

-   Be a friendly face - sometimes just asking “How’s it going?” can make a big difference.

**Tips:**

-   Keep an eye out for teams that seem stuck or disengaged.

-   Offer encouragement and help teams celebrate small wins.

-   Know who to escalate issues to (e.g., organizers, tech/data volunteers).

| Group/project | Volunteer |
|-------------------------------------|-----------------------------------|
| Automated QA of Schools pupils nd their characteristics / SEN | Lauren Snaathorst |
| Developing a Historical School Identifier Dimension | Rich Bielby |
| Form to Report Generator | Lara Edington |
| New insights on User Analytics for EES | Cam Race |
| Persistent Absence Explorer (EES API) | Mohamed Zayed |
| Using LLMs for Third Line QA on Statistical Releases | Menna Zayed |
